JOINT VENTURES AND TEAMING ARRANGEMENTS — 8(a) JOINT VENTURE AGREEMENT CHECKLIST (SBA submission requirements per 13 CFR 124.513)

I. Documentation the managing 8(a) participant must submit to its servicing SBA District Office to approve a Joint Venture:
1. The Joint Venture Agreement (must meet all requirements of 13 CFR 124.513(c)).
2. Three years of signed company year-end financial statements and federal tax returns for EACH participant.
3. A letter from the managing 8(a) participant stating: (a) what the 8(a) participant lacks in capacity/capability that makes the JV necessary; (b) what specific benefits (beyond contract revenue) the 8(a) firm receives and how; (c) what resources the 8(a) participant brings to the JV.

II. Required provisions IN the Joint Venture Agreement (every JVA for an 8(a) contract, including Mentor-Protégé):
1. Set forth the purpose of the JV.
2. Designate an 8(a) participant as managing venturer, and an employee of the managing venturer as the project manager responsible for performance.
3. State that not less than 51% of net profits go to the 8(a) participant.
4. Provide for a special bank account in the JV's name requiring all parties' signatures for withdrawals; all contract payments deposited there, all expenses paid from it.
5. Itemize all major equipment, facilities, and resources furnished by each party with a detailed schedule of cost/value.
6. Specify responsibilities for contract performance, source of labor, and negotiation.
7. Obligate all parties to ensure performance and complete it despite any member's withdrawal.
8. Keep accounting/administrative records at the managing venturer's office (unless District Director approves elsewhere).
9. Require final original records retained by the managing venturer upon completion.
10. Require quarterly financial statements (cumulative receipts/expenditures incl. principals' salaries) to SBA within 45 days of each operating quarter.
11. Require a project-end profit-and-loss statement incl. final profit distribution to SBA within 90 days of completion.

III. Supplemental information to submit with the JVA:
1. General identifying info: JV name; JV EIN; JV business credit report (formerly DUNS) number; participating firm names; for each firm — primary industry, current capabilities, brief history, SBA Form 355 (size determination), SBA Form 1623 (debarment/suspension certification).
2. Project description: customer agency; size/dollar value; type (general construction, janitorial, etc.); expected award date; duration/performance requirements; offer & acceptance letters; draft/final bid proposal; cash-flow/personnel/equipment/facility analysis; Statement of Work; the procurement's synopsis.
3. For non-8(a) participants: number/value of contracts in the procurement's NAICS code; relevant experience and licenses; details of all previous JVs with 8(a) firms.
4. Each 8(a) participant indicates the JVA's effect on existing contracts (cash flow, personnel, equipment, facilities).
5. Outline profit distribution demonstrating the combined 8(a) participants receive at least 51%.
6. Demonstrate the JV meets size requirements; analyze percentages of work by each firm and subcontractor (13 CFR 124.510, 124.513(d), 125.6).
7. Financial: special-account documentation; capital amount/percentage each venturer contributes; three years of financials/tax returns per participant; bonding evidence where appropriate.
8. Employees: list needed by position/type/number; number and skills supplied by each venturer; hiring/management responsibilities.
9. Project management: how it is handled (managing 8(a) participant must maintain control); specific performance/labor/negotiation responsibilities; breakdown of work tasks by venturer.
10. Certifications: that all required provisions are in the JVA (13 CFR 124.513(c)); that the JVA is fair and equitable and substantially benefits the 8(a) participant (with explanation); that the 8(a) participant brings substantial value beyond its 8(a) status.
